steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
The numerator starts with $-b = -11$, which eliminates those choices (they have $+11$).

Calculate the discriminant:
$$b^2 - 4ac = 11^2 - 4(1)(-14) = 121 + 56 = 177$$
Substituting into the quadratic formula:
$$x = \frac{-11 \pm \sqrt{177}}{2}$$
That confirms D is correct.
